questionHtml: Formulate half-equations for the reactions at the anode (negative electrode) and cathode (positive electrode) during discharge of a lithium-ion battery.
markschemeHtml:
Anode (negative electrode):

Li (graphite) → Li+ (electrolyte) + e–

OR

LiC6 (s) → 6C (s) + Li+ (electrolyte) + e– ✔

 

Cathode (positive electrode):

Li+ (electrolyte) + e– + MnO2 (s) → LiMnO2 (s)

OR

Li+ (electrolyte) + e– + NiO2 (s) → LiNiO2 (s)

OR

Li+ (electrolyte) + e– + CoO2 (s) → LiCoO2 (s)

OR

2Li+ (electrolyte) + 2e– + 2CoO2 (s) → Co2O3 (s) + Li2O (s) ✔

 

Accept “polymer” for “electrolyte”.

Award [1 max] if electrodes are reversed.

Do not accept “CO” for “Co”.

meta.title: Formulate half-equations for the reactions at the anode (negative electrode) and cathode (positive electrode) during discharge of a lithium-ion battery.
breadcrumbLabels: C.6 Electrochemistry, rechargeable batteries and fuel cells (HL only)
